matches.empty: No matches found. Click “Refresh Matches” to fetch your recent games.
matches.no_applicable_goals: No applicable goals for this match.

analysis.no_data: No data for the selected filters.
analysis.no_data_hint: Make sure you have parsed matches with data at {minutes} minutes.

goals.empty: No goals yet. Use the form above to create your first goal.

challenges.title: Weekly Challenges
challenges.subtitle: Accept a challenge each week to push your limits
challenges.choose: Choose one of this week’s challenges:
challenges.no_challenges: No challenges available yet. Play some matches first to generate challenges.
challenges.history_empty: No challenge history yet. Complete some challenges to see them here!
challenges.desc_low_deaths: Die {n} times or less in one game
challenges.desc_hero_damage: Deal {n}+ hero damage in one game
challenges.desc_low_deaths_games: Die {n} or fewer times in {g} games
challenges.desc_hero_damage_total: Deal {n}+ total hero damage this week
challenges.desc_cs_at_10_avg: Average {n}+ CS at 10 minutes across {g} games

mental_health.title: Mental Wellbeing
mental_health.disabled_body: Enable post-game mood check-ins in Settings → Mental Wellbeing to start building your history.
mental_health.need_more: Complete more check-ins to see your mood trends.
mental_health.history_empty: No check-ins yet. After your next match a prompt may appear based on your frequency setting.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your application settings
settings.db_location_desc: Your Dota 2 match data and goals are stored in a SQLite database.
settings.logs_desc: Debug logs for troubleshooting API calls and background parsing. Verbosity is controlled by the RUST_LOG environment variable (default: debug).
settings.suggestion_difficulty_desc: Controls how ambitious your CS goal suggestions will be. Medium targets 5–10% improvement over your recent average.
settings.analytics_desc: Help improve Dota Keeper by sending anonymous usage data. We track page views, feature usage, and errors to understand how the app is used.
settings.analytics_collect_detail: App version, platform, page views, feature usage events (e.g. “goal created”, “challenge accepted”).
settings.analytics_not_collect_detail: Steam ID, match IDs, hero choices, goal descriptions, or any personally identifiable information.
settings.backfill_desc: Download and parse 100 matches from before your oldest stored match. This helps build up your historical data for better analysis and trends.
settings.backfill_warning: ⚠️ This process may take several minutes as it fetches and parses each match individually.
settings.bg_parse_desc: Automatically parse unparsed matches in the background at startup — one at a time, every 10 seconds — so your history is always up to date without manual intervention.
settings.reparse_desc: Retry parsing all matches that failed or haven’t been parsed yet. This is useful for matches that couldn’t be parsed initially due to API issues.
settings.reparse_warning: ⚠️ This will attempt to parse all unparsed/failed matches, which may take several minutes.
settings.clear_matches_desc: Permanently delete all match data from the database. This will remove all matches and their associated parsed data.
settings.clear_matches_warning: ⚠️ WARNING: This action cannot be undone! All match history will be permanently deleted.
settings.updates_desc: Manually check for a new version of Dota Keeper at any time.
settings.mental_checkin_desc: Get occasional prompts to reflect on how you felt during a session. Helps you spot patterns like fatigue or frustration before they affect your gameplay.
settings.mental_checkin_local: All data is stored locally on your device only. You can skip any check-in, and disable this at any time.
settings.frequency_desc: Controls how frequently the post-game mood prompt appears. Loss streaks always trigger a check-in regardless of this setting.
settings.clear_mood_desc: Permanently delete all mood check-in history. Your match data and goals are not affected.
settings.logout_desc: Log out from your Steam account. Your match data and goals will remain stored locally and will be available when you log back in.
settings.factory_reset_desc: Delete all data and reset the app to its initial state. Useful for testing the first-run experience.
settings.factory_reset_warning: ⚠️ This permanently deletes all matches, goals, settings, and check-ins. The app will close immediately.
settings.modal_checkin_body: Occasionally, after a session, we’ll ask how you felt — using simple emoji scales. This helps you spot patterns like fatigue or frustration before they affect your gameplay.
settings.modal_checkin_li1: All data stays on your device
settings.modal_checkin_li3: Disable tracking at any time from this page

onboarding.step1_sub: What do you want to get out of it?
onboarding.goal_track_desc: Set targets and see if I’m hitting them
onboarding.goal_analyse_desc: Understand trends in my CS, KDA, win rate
onboarding.goal_mental_desc: Spot tilt patterns and avoid burnout
onboarding.step2_sub: Pick up to {max} heroes you play most. ({selected}/{max} selected)
onboarding.heroes_hint: You can always update favourites in the Analysis page.
onboarding.step3_sub: After some sessions we’ll ask 2 quick questions — like how calm you felt. It takes 5 seconds and helps you spot burnout before it hits your rank.
onboarding.feature_local: All data stays on your device
onboarding.feature_disable: Disable tracking at any time in Settings
onboarding.step4_sub: Backfilling gives Dota Keeper historical data to build better goal suggestions and trend analysis.

analytics_consent.title: Help Improve Dota Keeper
analytics_consent.intro: Would you like to help improve Dota Keeper by sending anonymous usage data?
analytics_consent.collect_4: Error events (type only, no personal details)
analytics_consent.not_collect_1: Steam ID or any personally identifiable information
analytics_consent.not_collect_2: Match IDs, hero choices, or game data
analytics_consent.not_collect_3: Goal descriptions, targets, or any content you enter
analytics_consent.note: You can change this preference anytime in Settings → Privacy.

feedback.title: Send Feedback
feedback.category_label: What kind of feedback is this?
feedback.positive: Works great — tell us what you like
feedback.prompt_bug: What happened? What did you expect?
feedback.prompt_feature: What were you trying to do that wasn’t possible?
feedback.prompt_positive: What specifically do you like? Why is it useful?
feedback.priority_label: How much is this affecting your use of the app?
feedback.priority_blocking: Blocking — I can’t use the app properly
feedback.toast_error: Failed to submit feedback. Please try again.
